title: Excellent balance of comfort, sound quality and build for indoor/outdoor use
text:
Update - 4 Jan 18: I've watched several YouTube videos and haven't experienced any audio lag of the sort reported by many reviewers. As below, I've applied the firmware update 1.3.2 and it seems to have resolved several of the problems. One issue I have noticed is that when connected to a device, e.g., an iPhone, the buds won't connect to a different device, e.g., my MacBook, unless I manually break the connection. This is annoying and shouldn't happen.

I ordered and received these as soon as they were available, so my review reflects ~ 2 months of use. I did not receive a promotional pair or discount from Bose for this review. I also own Bose QC35 noise cancelling headphones that are used on long flights to create a serene environment. These are not, nor did I expect them to be, noise cancelling. Some reviewers have comments that these buds didn't offer noise cancellation, but Bose never claimed that they did or would.

My first advice to anyone buying the SoundSp

title: Good wireless headphone at a price
text:
I am using an iPhone 7 plus and miss having the headphone cord plug in. I have been thinking of getting the Apple wireless AirPods but they are always out of stock and I have not been a big fan of the corded headphones that come with iPhones. I have Bose noise cancelling and Bose corded in ear headphones that I have always liked. I like the sound Bose creates on their headphones and speakers so I was excited to try these new wireless sport headphones.

Unboxing the headphones has a high end feel with quality packaging and quality feel of the headphones and case. I really like the headphone case for these. The case protects the headphones and charges them. The headphones can last up to 5 hours with one charge and the case has capacity to charge them 2 more times for an additional 10 hours of play. That is great feature when on a long flight where 5 hours of charge may not be enough. You charge the has through usb connection and the case shows power left through front l

title: Worth every dime.
text: Bought these a few months back and purposely waited to write a review. I’ll address the one thing everyone seems to bring up; sound dropping from the left ear. Admittedly, it has happened about a half dozen times in the last three months, but it only lasted for a couple seconds before it came back in. Usually it happened when moving from inside to outside. Otherwise this is a five-star product. They stay put during vigorous workouts, are comfortable, and the sound quality is as good as you’d expect from Bose.

title: ABSOLUTE GARBAGE!!!!
text: My original review was rejected by Amazon, so I'm just gonna say that these were a massively expensive disappointment. Because they are Bose, my expectations were high, and I was ready for an awesome experience. Unfortunately, that was not to be the case. These things are absolutely garbage and not even remotely worth the money you have to pay for them.

title: Prepare to be Disappointed
text:
These wireless headphones are not worth the price! I bought these because I wanted to replace the Airpods I bought since they were too painful.
The only pro to these earbuds was that they had a small size. However, everything else wasn't that great.
For one, they are extremely bulky. But the main reason why I returned these was the sound quality. I thought for the price that I was paying and since it was a Bose product, I would be amazed by the sound quality. Sadly, the cheap, no-name wired earbuds that I bought on Amazon for $8 has much better sound quality than these.

title: They are decent, comparable to AirPods and Liberty+
text:
I held off on buying these for a year or more because of all the negative reviews referencing connection issues. I have used them half a dozen times now over the last couple of weeks and I haven’t really had connection issues. I have had some brief cutouts when I hold my phone in my left hand and go for a walk. If I cup my hand around either earbud it does cut out, I only did this for testing purposes based on other reviews, not sure what exactly this tells you.
I also have AirPods and Zolo Liberty+. So I tested each of them the same way. One hand cupped over an ear does not make either of them cut out, but two hands (one over each ear) does. So I find it slightly disappointing that the higher dollar and more well known audio brand Bose has inferior signal strength.
Ease of connectivity is good. Although brand new out of the box it prompted me to simply open the Bose app to connect and setup.. the app just hung trying to connect. I killed the app and went to the Bluet

title: Bose Sound, Bose Quality
text:
I should preface this, there seemed to be some problems when these first came out. You can read some early reviews. The first thing I did with my first pair was download the firmware update. This seems to have fixed things. I've had no problems. My new pair already had the firmware update.

There was a sale, $50 off, I really like my first pair, so now I have two, or four. For me, the earbuds fit firmly in my ears. The controls are easy to use--Bose has always put reasonable controls on their Bluetooth Phone/Buds. The paring is usually easy, although they don't seem to like my Surface Pro. The microphone/headset/telephone part works very well. Oh, and the sound? I have no particular expertise, but I like Bose. So I bought another pair. And my excuse for two? I can sometimes misplace them, so for me, finding Bright Orange is easier than finding black. Well done, Bose! Highly recommended.

title: I feel like a antenna broadcasting signals to everyone LOL
text:
Where do I begin, well I ordered these while under the influence thinking. "Hey bose is a great company lets check these out."
Ordered, arrived next day, "what the hell is this and when did I order it..?"
Whatever, downloaded the app connected them 'locked' or twisted or whatever into my ears, really weird. Thought hey these are pretty good... not bad at all.

SOUND
I am also an AirPod owner and wanted to compare the two, so I got two iPhones, synced the audio and listened at the same time. Same amazing quality for both, actually indistinguishable. I am no apple fan boy by any means, I let the product speak for itself.
COMFORT
Seeing as they bost sounded the same next was the comfort, AirPods won on this category as they are just more i guess slimming or light weight. The Bose felt like there was just something coming out of my ear like an antenna lol I don't know how to describe it.
APPEARANCE
This is actually hilarious, I w

title: Great Sound
text: I have had MANY bluetooth headsets over the years. I have two now that are my go to sets, but one is the neck band type and the other has the connecting wire. I wanted to go completely wireless and did quite a bit of research. I had it narrowed down to these Bose and the Jabra Elite Active 65t. Reviewers seemed to give the sound edge to the Bose, so I went that way. And, I'll agree that sound is impressive. It's the best sound of any BT headset I've ever tried. It has great sounding bass with good mids and highs. In fact I had to set my EQ to all nominal to take some bass out. The buds are quite large and do stick out of your ears much more than others, but they're very comfortable. I have a large tip in my right ear and a medium in my left. I'd suggest using the largest tip that's comfortable for you to increase sound quality, isolation and secure fitness. Battery life appears to be as advertised and I like the magnetic charging case, some cases (even at higher price points) don't sec

title: Incredible sound!
text: I bought these earphones after months of contemplating the purchase. The reviews are not particularly good. I use wireless earphones 4 or 5 hours a day both listening to music and books. I have been through 5 pair that cost about $30 each. They seemed acceptable for a short while and then fell apart despite stellar reviews. The sound was ok. The cords behind my neck were constantly caught up in my hair. I was really reluctant to spend $200 even for the Bose name. So I opened the package carefully, saved the shipping box and expected to return them after a couple weeks. The first thing I noticed was the sound. The quality is amazing. There is no comparison to the others. I feel I am in a concert hall. I still expected problems. The idea of no cords was very appealing and I enjoy that tremendously. They are very comfortable and stay in my ears even when I am laying down. I use them while exercising and they stand up to a lot of sweat. They  connect easily and very quickly to both my phon

title: Stick with the regular Bose SoundSport Version
text:
Tried these, tired AirPods, still using my previous generation Bose SoundSport; returned these and the AirPods.

I currently have the Bose SoundSport headphones but I'm on my second pair and they're starting to see some wear. I use them 5 or 6 days a week while I run or work out. I also use them for phone calls at work and for personal use. In my opinion, they are some of the best Bluetooth headphones you can buy. [[ASIN:B01L7PSJFO Bose SoundSport Wireless Headphones, Black]]

I took a chance on AirPods. Terrible. Returned the second day. When running on the treadmill, the one side kept falling out of my ear. Fun fact - human ears aren't exactly the same. Even between your left and right ears. So while one side fit fairly snug, the other kept falling out. Yeah, you can buy silicone attachments to help keep them securely in your ear, but then you have to remove and store them to use the AirPod charging case.

On to the Bose SoundSport Free. These fit more s

title: Phone Calls Only Play Out of the Right Ear Piece
text:
I ordered a pair of the Bose SoundSport Free after having a pair of the SoundSport Pulse headphones which have a connecting wire between the two earphones. The Pulse headphones play both music and phones calls in stereophonic sound, meaning you hear the output in both earphones.

The new SoundSport Free headphones play music out of both earpieces but phone calls come out of only the right side. Completely unacceptable in my opinion for a product costing $250. Appple's Airpod product - costing a $100 less - plays phone calls out of both earpieces.

Fingers crossed that Bose addresses this issue with some form of firmware update.

title: please don't buy!
text: I originally thought these were great, and then the left one stopped working after the second use.  many emails and attempts to resolve the issue only left me sending them back.  I received a replacement, which worked for 3 workouts, and now the same issue.  I have wasted HOURS trying to get these to work.  I am now sitting waiting for a huge update to apply.  It's be 20 minutes and it is only 20 percent done, after the second attempt.  For the price, these should have been magical.  It is clear they rushed to market with these.  Looks like back to the apple store to pay less for better working ones.

title: Not ready for prime time
text: So the sound for music is great. But these drop out wayyyy too much. Also, you can only use the right earbuds on calls, and even those have had a good deal of static. Pretty surprised this is a Bose product. I have other products but this one will be going back.

title: Five Stars
text: I love this!!! From a frequent traveler!

title: Do not buy if one of the devices you want to use is a Windows PC/Laptop
text: Meets the high standard I have come to expect from Bose on sound quality, but fails to meet promoted performance. Works great with Cell but does not work in stereo with any windows pc. one ear bud works when connected to Windows PC. Bose sadly does not own up to single bud activity. Bose suggest it is a Microsoft driver issue, but every other wireless speaker I have connected to Windows, including  apple airbuds work perfectly with stereo, Bose is the only one not working. Do not buy these if you want to use them with a Windows pc in addition to your cell. The only reason I did not rate 2, is bcse it is a 5 for cell phone, but advertised to be able to connect to 7 devices, certainly at least one of them will be a computer.

title: After a cuple of months broke not very resistant to sweat.
text:
the Bose have a very unique sounds they are very good quality sound headphones loud but not piercing just wright make sure you put the right size ear plugs they go above and beyond drewing exercise with stands the abuse the sweat the wet. They also dont get interference like the jay birds do when your in the gym also you can have one ear bud working while the other is put away for you guys that like to work with ear buds but dont wanna get caught .
July 5 2020
Last night worked out long walk sweat and first time i have a pair of good quality headphones broke on me because of sweat... no resistance at all .great sound great technology but after giving it a normal usage it fails 2 satisfy consumer under normal wear and tear of exercising like I normally do first time a pair of headphones break because of sweat how disappointing I thought these were going to last at least a year or more

title: Connectivity issues are a fatal flaw
text: These were touted as amazing by most professional reviewers but unfortunately that is not the case. I bought them specifically to use with my old ipod nano at the gym and secondarily for use watching video downloads on my ipad. Alas they cut out frequently from the ipod when using the eliptical machines but also just walking around the gym! At first I thought this was due to the fact that my ipod nano cannot download the bose app but now I see 100s of actual users had the same problem. Have not had this issue with my ipad but then again I typically watch movies when I am stationary on a plane or relaxing on my bed. Very sad as I would like to go cordless but I'm not paying $169 for earbuds that literally chop up every song when they cut out!

title: Do Not Waste Your Money!!
text: Sound is great when it works.  The left ear bud constantly dropped and would not connect.  It was frustrating to get it to work properly. I only had sound in the right ear bud.  I was waiting for this product to come out because BOSE makes great products but these SoundSports are utterly disappointing. I returned them. I was not going to pay $250 to listen to music in one ear or sometimes two ears when it felt like working. Read the comments on the BOSE website about how many times the left ear bud disconnected. It isn't my pair....as the rep told me it was....it is a very much flawed design.  Do not waste your time and money. You will regret it.

title: Doesn't work with Apple watch series 3
text: Doesn't work with Apple watch series 3

title: battery life is horrible, does not hold a charge
text: It was working great for the first few months...then I started noticing the battery would be dead the next day after a full recharge. Definitely not worth the $$$$, my cheap $20 earbuds were much more reliable.

title: These Sound Amazing and They're Comfy!
text:
Got these on the Prime Day deal on Monday, delivered Tuesday so I tested them out on a run - these things sound great. They are sticking in my ear nicely... also sticking out a little - I probably look a little silly (see Shrek references in other reviews) but that doesn't bother me - loving them after day 1.

If I run into any issues or the fit starts to loosen up, I will update this review.

For now, 5 stars!

title: Not sure how these ever passed quality control
text:
I run marathons, and enjoy listening to music while running as well as before hockey games. It came time for a new set, and these stood out because I prefer wireless, but also there’s no wire between each earpiece. I ended up hating these within 15 minutes!

Pros: good fit, great sound quality

Cons: these lose connection. ALOT. The right ear connects to the phone, left ear connects to the right ear. Well the left ear loses connection constantly, reconnecting randomly. In the end I had more silence than music. After about 45 minutes, silence/music was replaced by a buzzing sound like “dead air” on a radio and it never recovered from this. The right ear lost connection about every 5 minutes on average (once per song), and once dropped connection three times during a single song!

Reading other reviews this apparently is a constant issue, even after a 1 hour firmware update immediately done after purchasing. How Bose is selling these, especially f

title: Sound crystal clear, but they discount too much
text: Even though they sound crystal clear, I'm not a fan of the earbuds.  I am not a fan of the earbuds, because they randomly cut out.  I love the fact that they are truly wireless earbuds, but for as much as they cut out, they weren't worth the money.  At $200, I expected a lot better quality.  Especially considering that they were originally priced at $250.  I have tried three pairs of wireless headphones, now, and have had bad luck with all three pair.  I originally tried Earin ear buds, where the left earbud would randomly cut out.  I also tried Jabra Sport earbuds, which worked great for a few months, but randomly stopped charging.  I have come to conclusion that the concept of truly wireless headphones is ahead of its time, for the technology is not there to support it, especially considering a reputable company like Bose is struggle to make headphones that maintain a connection.

title: Perfect Perfect
text: As always Bose quality....Perfect Perfect Perfect

title: The sound quality is great. A major improvement from the Bose Sound sport ...
text: The sound quality is great. A major improvement from the Bose Sound sport wireless and much better than the apple AirPods. They even got the fit perfect, they do not fall out. However there are major flaws. Connectivity is bad with frequent drops in the left earbud with head turning. There is a lag in the audio when watching videos. Charging is not consistent despite them being in the charging case. I am doubtful all of these will be fixed by firmware updates.

title: Sound Quality and fit simply the BEST!
text: I love all electronics and can't wait to try them out when new headphones launch. I bought the Jabra Elite 65t. At first I thought wow these are pretty great. After wearing them at work for 2 hours or so they started hurting my ears. A co-worker of mine said to try the Bose Soundsport Free as you can't beat them for comfort. I disliked the fact that they stick out so far. I do also have Apple Airpods which look silly lol. I bought the Bose and after one day immediately returned the Jabra. The sound is so much fuller, more bass and the comfort is amazing. They do stick out a bit further than Jabra but the sound quality is totally worth the comprise. I still use my airpods as they just work flawlessly but not as comfortable or sound as good. I would have to say right now Bose has nailed it for wireless earpods. The only thing that I don't like as much is if someone walks up to my desk and I take them off they continue to play whereas the Jabra's automatically pause. I have had no issues

title: Bst sounding wireless buds
text: This is the best sounding wireless headphone (buds) I've tried.  Love them!  The storage/charging case is great.  I take them off, put them in the case and they charge automatically.

title: Excellent Earphones That Need Minor Improvements
text:
Setup

Right out of the box, it was easy to connect the BSF to my iPhone X. This really shouldn't be a big deal, but unfortunately, I have owned many headphone/earphones that make the pairing process troublesome. Some won't connect the first time you start the process; others will connect and then not receive any audio, which then requires unpairing and re-pairing. Anyway, I'm glad there was no hiccup about this.

Initial Sound

I purposefully selected a hip/hop track from Apple Music, something I believed would start with a good amount of bass. A lot of people on YouTube and reviews here said they liked the bass response on the BSF. Consequently, I wanted to test that right out the gate. And I was impressed. The bass hit hard! I have the Beats Studio Wireless 3 that I use for workouts at the gym. They are fairly good headphones, and I have been pretty happy with them. But the bass on the Beats over the ear headphones is simply different from the

title: Good earphones, I think 3.5/5 is more accurate
text:
Setting these up with new devices is a breeze. The sound quality is great and despite their size and protrusion, they stay in my ears despite running or working out and don't feel heavy at all. It feels like I have nothing in my ears. I'm pleasantly surprised and impressed with the sound quality and even though it's not noise canceling, I can't hear things around me well at all, which is an added bonus. In addition, I do not experience any lag with videos.
The performance is usually very consistent but I have noticed that with some movements or positions, it loses connection with the left bud but I can usually tell when this will happen and bring it back so I'm sure it's not a real problem with the buds, just a minor inconvenience. A bigger inconvenience is that the case battery seems to leak and not hold charge so if you don't use them for a few days, make sure you check the battery before using as the case may have run out. I have reached the gym several times after not using th

title: Disconnects frequently. Very difficult to reconnect.
text:
They fit nicely in the ear. They sound good on calls. They have great sound when listening to music/video. They only play phone calls, including zoom meetings, in ONE ear, the right earbud. This annoyed me but I was working toward getting over it. I pay for yoga classes on zoom and my kids are loud, so it only plays the "zoom" class in one ear because it determines it's a phone call? Not sure how they make this determination. It is definitely more difficult to hear phone calls when they only play in one ear, and they should fix this. I have never had a headset that only played calls in one ear, and if it can play music and video in 2 ears, it should play calls that way too.

HOWEVER, the connection issues are what tanked my relationship with these earbuds. I'll give you an example. This morning I went for a run. My phone alarm went off reminding me of something. All of a sudden my podcast was no longer playing through my ear buds. I stopped running. I wasted 10 minutes trying

title: Kind of disappointed
text:
The original pair I received came in an opened box and when I attempted to connect them, they had already been paired to someone else’s phone. I didn’t know if those were broken/refurbished or if anything was missing from the packaging so they had to go back. Especially for the price I paid. THANK YOU Amazon for the quick fix!

Now, I did a lot of research and tried a few different earbuds and headphones before I decided to give these a try. I expected a lot considering they’re Bose and the price. The sound when playing music is excellent. A+ in the sound department! The lag when watching Netflix or another type of online movie is VERY disturbing. NEGATIVE! For phone calls, they work & connect quickly, but there’s an annoying, ongoing buzzing sound on my end. My callers hear an echo and complain about the background noise being louder than me. Not good. The fit is average. I haven’t used them for a workout yet because they’re a bit loose for just my normal usage. I’m using th

title: Awesome, if in quiet room
text:
DARN! left bud dropped out terribly often. Had to return to Amazon. Too annoying to buy another. Bose Customer support not able to help and the app was really bad.
Great sound and fit right out of the box that clearly states, " Made for iPhone, iPad, iPod ".
Other than buttons are hard to use, I have zero complaints using with iPhone 7 plus. Perhaps those that try to use these with Android or windows devices, which they are clearly NOT MADE FOR, have most of the complaints?
I'm happy with my purchase and accept that Bose quality is very expensive. You get what you pay for. I'm hearing aspects of sound I didn't previously know existed with ear buds.
Update 12/19
Sound is great provided I am in a quiet room. On the street, coffee shops, airplanes and so forth my $20.00 Anker wireless ear buds out perform these expensive Bose units. I had to buy a $350.00 set if Sony noise canceling headphones to finally get the sound I needed. I still use the Bose earbuds

title: Wait for the next version.  An unexpected and serious disappointment.
text:
I found Bose's latest wireless headphones to be a fairly serious disappointment, which was surprising and frustrating after having bought 5 or 6 pairs of headphones over the years, along with speakers and a Wave Radio. I *like* Bose products.

Let me start with the positive, to be fair.

1. They sound great.
2. They stay in my ears. (A feature Apple did not think to include with their glorious AirPods.)
3. The Bose connect app on the iPhone is slick and useful.

The negatives.

1. They are very big and jut from my ears in a way that looks comical. I've seen other people compare them to the bolts on Frankenstein's neck, but for the ears. Actually, though, it's worse than that, because their weight makes them droop. So I'd describe the look as more Frankenstein Eeyore. If you have hair that covers your ears, you will probably be ok. But I am close to bald, so I can't really wear them in public. Since I bought them to wear during m

title: Great sound...when they work
text:
I have had these since October. The sound quality is GREAT! Cancels noise around me when they are in my ears. Battery is OK but not even close to what they say. Maybe 4 hours with the charge in between. Mine decided to rename themselves and then sometimes decide not to connect. Kind embarrassing when I start the music and it's blaring from my phone and everyone around can hear but I can't and realize they didn't connect again.

Update have had these over a year and seriously the best headphones ever. After upgrades and set up again on my iPhone I have not had any troubles with sound or connection. Battery lasts a long time and headphones never disconnect.

title: Awesome - But Check!
text:
I love these babies! It's been about 2 years since I first saw them advertised (they may have been available before that) and I've wanted them ever since. But it's a very hefty pricetag for me, so I needed time to save up and justify the purchase. When it was a Amazon Prime Day Deal, I knew I needed to pounce right away. And I'm so glad I did!!

I do a lot of work at my parents' farm involving cutting branches, hauling rocks, working with animals, etc, where wires can get so easily tangled. So the wireless aspect is what I really wanted. But most ear buds don't actually stay in my ears unless I'm holding perfectly still. Bose are the exception. I can wear these for hours without them slipping or getting uncomfortable, even when the sweat is pouring down in 89% humidity. The sound quality is outstanding (no surprise, since they are a Bose product). The controls are easy to use and let you play/pause, adjust the volume, and disconnect/connect to bluetooth. The charging case is

title: Four Stars
text: Great if you want to look like you have a cockroach hanging out of your ear

title: Good sound quality and stays in my ears well.
text: I use these earbuds several times a week and really like them. They stay in my ears better than my previous set of earbuds from a different manufacturer. The sound quality is good and the Bluetooth connection stays solid, unlike an LG set that I used to have. The battery life is ok. I can't figure out how to just turn them off. I out them back into the charging case, but since they don't ever go off, they just run down the charging case. A different brand that I had would turn off, then recharge in the case until full and then the case continued to hold a charge since it was not continually charging the earbuds. If I went several days without using them they would still have a charge. The Bose earbuds will be dead after a few days of no use if I do not plug them into the USB charger. It's not a huge deal since I've gotten used to them, but it is annoying.

title: Sounds amazing
text:
As always Bose makes and amazing product , these for being Bluetooth is a very great product I tried a lot of wireless bluetooth’s out there and fortunately these where the ones I bought. They do stick out but I don’t care , these are just an amazing product . When I make calls they hear me clear even when I speak low..
the music is outstanding with that bass , unlike some other bluetooths you don’t get a good quality sound like these , worth the money thanks Bose 😃

title: Not worth the pricing
text:
I've owned these since May 2019 and I would say that for almost a year they were fantastic. I use them frequently and they performed well....until the one year mark. My ear buds will sporadically not charge / not connect to the charging case - leaving me with uncharged buds when they appeared to be charging.

I've kept them clean and the software updated for the charging case as well. It's unfortunate for the price that I've had to troubleshoot so many issues.

For essentially $150-200/year for ownership, I would recommend a cheaper wireless device.

title: Not ready for prime time
text: I purchased these at Best Buy from a Bose salesperson.  The sound quality is typical Bose, which is great.  However, there are issues such as the left unit randomly cutting off, and the units not keeping a charge.  The charging station is extremy fickle, sometimes not charging at all.  Other times stopping at 90%.  I cannot recommend these at anywhere near this price.  Bose needs to go back to the drawing board with these.

title: Great set of ear buds
text: Love these. Not sure if I'm more excited about the fact the case charges the ear buds or that you can locate them if you misplace them. They stay in my ear very well. Three sizes to choose in the pack to see which fits your ear.

title: Traditional Headphone Sound in Earbuds?
text:
Believe it or not, Bose approaches that achievement. This model has been released for close to a year and a half now yet no other leading, high-end wireless (truly or not) earbud to date has matched the sound quality on all three levels. The balance is superb.

They protrude because they are larger units (hence the best-in-class sound drivers and battery life). Two thumbs up from this self-proclaimed audiophile. Bonus points: although they are larger, they were able to fit under my snowboard mask comfortable.

title: Great
text: Love them.  I have small ears and nothing is very comfortable but these work for me!

title: Longtime Bose fan, not so much now.
text:
Ive been purchasing bose products for over 30 years. Was expecting the same and quality I have grown accustom to. Love the sound and fit. Battery life is abysmal. I can get maybe 2 hours of listening at work before I get the low battery warning. This is coming from a 100% charge.
And of course there is no recourse for getting this fixed. What a joke.

title: Five Stars
text: Absolutely love it! COMBO SET with apple watch best of the best for your sport.

title: Great Ear Buds!!!
text: My wife got these for me for Christmas (I had been eye balling them for a while and was fairly sure I wanted them). As soon as we were done with all the gift opening, I put them on the charger and came back later to test them out. I changed out the ear pieces to fit my ear and turned them on. Pairing them to my Samsung Note 9 was extremely easy and quick. I put them in my ears, made sure Dolby Atmos was engaged on my Note 9, and picked a movie and pushed play. The sound quality was great! I turned them up fairly loud and, true to Bose form, the lows sounded great. I was getting separate sounds to both ear buds. If someone was talking off screen to the left, I would get their voice in my left ear. If sound moved from one side to the other, in traveled from one ear, to both, to the other. Every now and then, I do get momentary disruption in the left ear, being that it is the secondary blue tooth device. However, it happens rarely and it is very momentary. Usually, it is because I am near

title: Not there yet don’t waste your money
text:
I was hoping this earphones work but the technology is not there yet pretty much all of these wireless free earbuds have the same cutoff sound issue. The left earbud will cut out and the right side. I dont think with all the issues these earbuds are worth more than $150 max.
After several replacement I decided to go with another brand. These didn’t work out for me the constant connection issues and video audio lag these are outdated. I got the Samsung galaxy buds for less money more features, wireless charging and more.

title: Second pair
text: I love my Bose headset this is my second pair after leaving the first pair on an airplane. *sigh*  The one note is that when listening to phone call this sound will only come through the right ear piece. Obviously when you’re listening to music....sound comes through both

title: Gifted
text: Bought these for my husband.  He says the sound quality is good as we have both known Bose to always be.  Only complaint so far is that twice he went out for a walk with them and could not get them to function.  Problem resolved.  I told him that before purchasing them I did read that with any problems first go to the app and make sure all updates on the product have been updated on his ear phones.  Have told him that both times he complained.  They are his earphones so whether he does that or not I have no idea.  He does use them almost daily.

title: Short battery life too expensive. Don't waste your money
text:
Battery life 2 hours. Discharges even when in charging pod. Short life get something cheaper and better designed.
These should be down in the low to mid range cost $30-40 max.

Typical crap product by BOSE I should have known better. Never buy Bose.

title: Great quality! Meh aesthetic...
text: Great quality material and sound! It sticks super far out of the ear is my only complaint...it doesn’t cause any problems with it falling out or anything (it hasn’t fallen out on me once) and it isn’t heavy so you forget about it until you see a mirror but basically it’s this really large thing sticking out of your ear that almost looks like a misplaced earplug or something...please work on the size! Otherwise a great product and if you don’t mind the appearance that much then I highly recommend!

title: Generally Awesome. Almost 5 stars.
text:
These don't seem like they will ever fall out of your ears. Awesome.

These sound WAY better than Apple's AirPods. I firmly believe these are a better buy than Apple AirPods. Again Awesome.

These sound better than PowerBeats and they don't have that silly cord between each ear. Again Awesome.

The price point is on par with other bluetooth headphones in this class. Mostly Awesome.

So what's not to like?

I've had these for about 5 days now and they do this funny thing. The sound occasionally cuts out from left to right. It's not often enough to be worried about it, but it happens. I just did a firmware update on mine so hopefully this will fix the problem and I'll be able to come back and call this a 5 star product.

title: Let down
text: I got my sound sports free and glad I did. The fit sucks anything that touches them why they are in my ears will make them fall out of my ears the wings don't help much. Bose, you let a lot of people down with the item.

title: Terrific headphones
text: These headphones are awesome. The sound is incredible and they fit my ears perfectly. I’ve only been using them for a couple of days but they never feel loose or like they’re going to fall out. The set-up was simple and so was registering them. I really like the carrying case/charger. The lights on the outside tell you him much battery life is left and it charges quickly with the micro USB. Definitely recommend.

title: Pretty good
text: These are pretty good. Sound quality is excellent, but they are a little big and do not fit my ears as well as my previous earbuds.

title: No sound from the left?!! What is this Bluetooth technology from the past decade?
text:
2 star on initial evaluation but 4 star if music sound quality is your number 1 priority.
Fits well and the sound quality is decent for music but a wireless Bluetooth headphone is only half done with just getting the sound right. I can't stand having a phone conversation with this set, it is so irritating not hearing out of the left ear because you have to raise the volume and it' very uncomfortable on the right ear!! I have a set of the Bose QC 20s, they are wired but, I have had excellent experience with them for the past 4 years which makes this headset all the more disappointing.

Okay here is the update: I ended up buying a set of the original Soundsport which is the predecessor to this model, you are welcome to see the review for that model, but here is a quick comparison.
Compared the Soundsport free
1. Sound quality for music: Win goes to Soundsport free. (It is noticeably better, however this offers decent sound quality for the price)
2. Range: Wi

title: Great for music but if you want to watch a movie, forget about it
text: I own a number of Bose products and I love them all, so I could not wait to get my hands on a pair of these. I opened the box and I was impressed with the quality. They felt very “Bose like”. So far, so good. I popped them in my ears and I realized I looked a little like Frankenstein with the way they poked out to the side, but I can give them a pass on that. Then I brought up some music and WOW, they sounded great. I tried to lower the volume and realized the volume rocker switch has no haptic feedback, so I had no idea if I was pushing it or not, I pressed harder than the volume dropped. Hmm, not good by maybe I can get used to that. However, I bought these to watch videos on a plane, so I brought up a movie on Netflix and that’s when the real problem started. The voice was so out of sync to the actor’s lips it was unbearable. I tried a little Hulu, same thing. I had read this could be an issue, but I thought surely a quick firmware update would fix the problem since I was certain th

title: Bose Soundsport Free
text: Great sound, love not being tangled in wires. Not crazy about master/slave connectivity because the sound can momentarily drop out until the master reconnects and then sound feels like a wave moving through your head. Not a major deal for me but it might bug some people. They fit well in my ears and don't fall out. I use them everyday to bike to and from work, while walking my dog.

title: Too expensive for the limited functionality; just get AirPods
text: For a $200 product, these are a major letdown. The fact that all calls, GoToMeeting-style apps etc only channel audio through the right ear is a major limitation and not excusable. It’s not that it’s “terrible” like others complain about; it’s just annoying enough to say “WTF were their engineers thinking when they accepted this major functionality compromise”. Sound quality is definitely worthy of the Bose name, but they aren’t loud/isolating enough to actually drown out noises like airplanes, the train (Chicago CTA drowns our their max volume output easily), street noises etc. I bought these instead of the Jabra Sport Elite (upgraded battery) and the AirPods, and within 30 minutes of use I regretted my purchase. If you want a dedicated sport bud, get the Jabras. If you want an all around bud, get the AirPods. The SoundSport Frees are squarely in the middle of doing nothing particularly well, and their limited functionality is NOT made up for by their sound quality. HARD PASS.

title: Great headphones for someone on the go
text: These headphones stay in your ears comfortably. You can run with them, work out, walk to work, etc. They have easy functions for calls and changing music. They pick up a lot of sounds and make it easy to do what you have to do while catching up with friends or listening to your favorite tunes. My only "complaint" would be that I wish the battery life lasted longer for those days you are on the phone a lot. You can still get in a long conversation and then put them back in the case to recharge. Great purchase. Love this limited edition color!

title: Great Sound but Battery drains to zero when not in use
text: I purchased when this first came out for $249.00 at Best Buy.  Size of unit is great sounds is crisp but Battery drains to zero when not in use. If you are an occasional user, this is NOT for you. If you are a daily user, this is a great product.

title: Incredible  Sound
text: These ear buds are the best. I had purchased several styles and a variety at various price points. None came close to the quality of sound which is standard for the Bose earbuds. The bass is incredible and the quality of  sound better than anything on the market. They are a little larger than some however the superior sound is worth it. They are a little pricier than some however again the price is justified.

title: Not worth $200, just wait for the Full Feature Amazon product to arrive in a few weeks
text:
I’m stuck with this expensive poor performer.
I returned this 2 times and now have to wait for a 3rd example of the average sounding non active noise blocking EXPENSIVE product That often didn’t link left and right channel.

Would never link to my new Samsung series 7 TV which is what I bought it for in the first place. A waste of money as Tribit ear buds work better for $69 not $199.

No refund, only exchange is possible so when the new one arrives I’ll just have to sell it unopened at a loss. AND I NEVER GOT THE 3rd replacement NO REFUND EITHER. WHY?

title: Great Sound and Features!
text: I was hesitant in buying these because of some of the reviews about how they sync with video but decided to take a chance when I found a GREAT deal and am not disappointed! There was a software update available through the Bose Connect app but these have great range, great sound, and no problems with video sync on my iPad Pro 12.9” or my iPhone 6S Plus. I am a professional Musican and college professor and the sound on these is pretty darn good. You still get some ambient noise but not as much as you would think. It doesn’t take long to charge the ear buds and if you have them in the case and the case needs to be charged, you can plug the case into the wall and the ear buds will charge first and then the case which is a nice feature since the case can continue to charge even after the ear buds come out. This is a pretty solid option. I use them on my stationary bike to watch movies or while working around the house. It is true that the phone calls come through the right ear only but I

title: These are the best ear buds ever
text: I bought my husband a pair in black at the Boise store and paid about $250. I paid over a $100 less for these and I love them.

title: Defective Left Bud
text: I am disappointed that Bose has a product being sold that has a defect and they are aware of it.  After receiving the product I spoke with Amazon customer service who in turn called the Bose customer service while I was on hold and it took a while for response.  However, once connected the Bose Rep indicated to amazon that the left bud is defective and there is no solution.  This is why I am returning there is no since in spending $150 on a product that you can't fully use.  And they or Amazon should take this off the market.  Period. I didn't mean to replace the item and will be returning the second pair as well.

title: Brilliant headphones
text: I have a very large collection of (expensive) headphones, all wired.  This is my first foray into wireless - I'm not disappointed. Really great sound.  Stay in my ears even while running or practicing yoga, but still comfortable after a longer use. Battery life is  ok - won't last a long flight, but you can recharge using the case for a 2nd try.  Haven't seen any sign of the dropouts mentioned in other reviews. Would buy again

title: Always disconnects the left ear bud and Bluetooth generally
text:
UPDATED: iPhone 12 Pro consistently drops or doesn’t connect, or one ear bud drops. Pathetic.

I love Bose but these things have horrible firmware. Constantly disconnecting or simply not playing audio, on an iPhone 11 Pro. Happens on my laptop too. Expected better.

title: bose needs better phone support
text: i love Bose. this product while ergonomically fantastic and sound as well. i sent the first p[air back. couldnt get them to fit my ears because i had the wrong ear pieces in the phones. i went to their store the next time after buying a $40 pair of ear phones. i sent them back too.  i talked to a real live human. got the right ear pieces. i went back a second time because they did not work properly for me.  i was shown how to use my phone as the controller. these little things hook right up to my phone Bluetooth. i can use the phones volume controls. i do not have to touch the controls on them. other folks  here said these wear out.

title: Solid buy.
text:
The headphones have a solid fit and finish (at $250 they should). The case is quite nice and the headphone stay in place via magnets.

Pros
​Easy setup with the Bose App.
Headphones fit snugly for long periods of time without causing ear fatigue.
Deep bass (almost too much when in quiet spaces).
Long battery life.
Take calls (right ear bud only)

Cons
​Expensive
Headphones stick out of your ears quite a bit.
No noise cancelling.
If you have $250 to spare and are in the market for very good Bluetooth ear buds these should be top of your list.

title: You pay for quality.
text:
So far these earphones are Superior in sound. Comfortable to wear. Have a apple
Airpod and cost less but sound was nothing like these. Airpod lasted exactly one year, and the sound volume is problematic.

title: Too Large-NO Independent usage
text:
The Bose SoundSports have a nice, clear sound, but they are VERY large! I purchased the Samsung Earbuds after these, and all I ever use are the Samsung's. The Bose SS HAVE to be used with at LEAST the Right bud everytime. Cannot be used independently. The Samsung's can. And I LOVE THAT.

Samsung earbuds are tiny and fit so well in my ears. I wanted these Bose SS's for over 3 years, and FINALLY got them on sale, but was disappointed. Sound quality is good, but still overall chose the Samsung's for size and independent usage. Battery life is better in Samsung's too.

title: Excellent gift for my daughter
text: My daughter really enjoys them. I’m glad I got them for her

title: Poor quality
text:
If you are trying to watch something ...it lags.

That didnt bother me too much because i mainly use it for music at the gym. However, the left ear bud goes in and out. Sometimes it works and sometimes it don't.

title: Surprising fail...
text: Floppy. Felt loose in my ears. Didn't keep a charge and one lasted longer than the other. Wouldn't always both sync at the same time. Super disappointed. I have a ton of other Bose products but this was an epic fail.

title: Great wireless headphones
text:
Great wireless headphones. Step up immediately from the wireless airpods, great looking, and wonderful fit.

Cons.: Experienced a problem with one of the headphones seeming to short out at times and stop working. Frustrating when on the go with these.

title: Must buy!
text: I always hated in ear earbuds but these are very comfortable. I wear them at hours on end and the never slip or get uncomfortable in my ear. The right one is only phone activated but the sound is amazing. Never dropped a call or worried I was too far away from my phone with Bluetooth connection it would lose signal and I live in the country. Great power case too and super fast! Love these earbuds

title: great sound
text: I love my new headphones! They are a bit big but the sounds are great and I then forget about the size, they hold a charge really long

title: Good option.
text: These are great!!  But. The only flaw. If you don’t use them the battery still drains.

title: Bose sound sports free
text: I use this for calling and music on my iPad and iPhone too
